content:
- completed story-20 task 02 end-to-end: flattened runner destination config to one explicit PostgreSQL target boundary, added shared runtime/config drift audits in `RunnerPublicContract`, updated runner fixtures/docs/tests, and renamed the runtime-plan conflict error to match the new target language
- required gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; `make test` initially failed on one outdated bootstrap-contract expectation after the error rename, which was fixed and then rerun green end-to-end
- task file and execution plan are marked complete, Ralph task switch has been executed, and the remaining step is to commit all changes (including `.ralph`) and push before quitting immediately

- finished and pushed story-20 task 02 in commit `14ff78d`: runner now owns one explicit PostgreSQL target boundary instead of `destination.connection`, runtime/config drift is locked down through shared `RunnerPublicContract` audits, and README/fixtures/contracts were updated to the flattened shape
- final required gates all passed before commit/push: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`; no `make test-long` run because this task did not change the ultra-long lane contract or selection
- Ralph task metadata is updated (`<status>done</status>`, `<passes>true</passes>`), the task switch hook already ran, and there is no next step in this context window except to quit immediately

- read previous progress, current active tasks, completed tasks, candidate task definitions, and the repo Makefile/Cargo workspace to determine the actual stack and selection context
- confirmed the project is a Rust workspace (`cargo`/`clippy`/`test`) with explicit GitHub workflow contract tests in `crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs`; all visible bug tasks already pass, and the only explicit active blocker is story-22a task 02 blocked by story-22a task 01
- selected story-21 task 01 as the next task because it is an unblocked failing task that unlocks the published-image path required by the later novice-user verification stories; next step after quitting is to work `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split.md`

- loaded prior progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` plus `tdd` skill instructions
- confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split.md`
- reading the task file next to determine whether this turn must create a plan or execute an approved plan

- read the active story-21 task file and confirmed it has no existing `NOW EXECUTE` or `TO BE VERIFIED` marker, so this turn must be a planning pass
- acceptance criteria focus on GitHub Actions build/test/publish behavior, multi-arch image publishing, main-only trusted publish paths, secret handling, and real authenticated workflow-log verification
- searching existing `.ralph/tasks` conventions now for how plan files and execution markers are represented in prior tasks

- inspected the current workflow/test surface: repo still has one `.github/workflows/master-image.yml` pipeline for runner + setup-sql only, built around several external actions and `master`-only triggers
- confirmed the repo now has three distinct image surfaces: root runner image, `crates/setup-sql` image, and verify image under `cockroachdb_molt/molt/Dockerfile`; downstream tests and README already treat published images as the novice-user contract
- discovered a planning risk: the task requires publish-on-`main`, but the current local and remote default branch is still `master`; the plan needs to make that branch-boundary migration explicit instead of assuming it away

- completed the planning pass for story-21 task 01 and created `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split_plans/2026-04-19-three-image-main-publish-workflow-plan.md`
- updated the task file with a `<plan>` path and marked the new plan `NOW EXECUTE`
- the plan makes three critical execution constraints explicit: replace the old `master-image` two-image boundary with one canonical three-image `main`-only workflow, flatten published image coordinates into one shared contract boundary, and require real hosted verification through the authenticated GitHub API/log path before the task can be considered done

- resumed Ralph for story-21 task 01 by reading prior progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries plus tdd skill instructions
- confirmed prior context already created a plan and likely flipped this task into execution mode; reading the task file and referenced plan now to verify the exact marker state and execution constraints
- next step is to inspect the relevant workflow/tests surface, then execute the plan with TDD tracer bullets and boundary cleanup if the plan still holds

- executed the first TDD tracer bullet for story-21 task 01: added one new ci contract for a canonical `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml` boundary on `main`
- forced a real RED with `cargo test -p runner --test ci_contract publish_images_workflow_triggers_only_on_pushes_to_main`, which failed because the new workflow file did not exist; then added the minimal workflow scaffold and reran the same test GREEN
- next step is to flatten the three-image published-image boundary and migrate the broader CI workflow contract off the old two-image `master-image` surface without scattering literals

- migrated the workflow contract tests from the old `master-image`/two-image shape to the new `publish-images`/`main`/three-image contract and rewrote the helper to own the new boundary cleanly
- replaced the workflow scaffold with a real main-only publish workflow: validate -> trusted publish, direct shell installs, multi-arch buildx pushes for runner/setup-sql/verify, explicit masking, and a published-image manifest artifact/output seam; updated README CI publish safety docs to match
- next step is boundary cleanup of legacy workflow references, deletion of the obsolete `.github/workflows/master-image.yml`, and then full repo gates (`make check`, `make test`) before attempting honest hosted verification

- `make check` passed cleanly after flattening workflow-only metadata out of `PublishedImageContract` so the shared image boundary no longer trips dead-code warnings in other test binaries
- `make test` reached a real README contract failure: the new CI publish safety copy mentioned contributor commands (`make check`/`make test`) in operator-facing docs; rewriting that section in user-facing language now and revalidating the targeted README plus CI contract tests
- next step after those targeted reds go green is to rerun the full `make test`, then move to honest hosted GitHub workflow verification if the local gates stay green

- completed the first honest hosted `main` push verification attempt using `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl`; GitHub created run `24639889644` for commit `1fd87b3` and failed it before any jobs started
- isolated the real hosted failure: `runner.temp` was used in top-level workflow `env`, which GitHub rejects before runner selection; converted that hosted red into a local ci-contract red by requiring `PUBLISHED_IMAGE_MANIFEST` to live in the `publish` job env, then fixed the workflow and reran the targeted contract green
- next step is to push the second `main` candidate with the manifest-path scope fix, inspect the new hosted run/jobs/logs, and continue until the hosted path is honestly green or proves blocked outside this turn

- pushed the hosted validation fix sequence to `main`: first moved the manifest path into a valid workflow context, then installed PostgreSQL tooling and exported its bin dir so hosted bootstrap tests can find `initdb`
- authenticated GitHub inspection now shows a materially different and healthier run shape: run `24640015556` is executing a real `validate` job instead of failing in workflow parsing, and earlier hosted logs proved the previous failure mode was missing Postgres binaries on the runner
- next step is to let the in-progress hosted validate lane finish, inspect its outcome/logs, and only then decide whether more hosted fixes are needed or whether task finalization can start

- latest local state on branch `main` is green for required local gates: `make check` passed and `make test` passed after adding hosted-only fixes for valid manifest-path scoping and PostgreSQL tooling installation in the validate job
- honest hosted verification progressed materially: run `24640015556` for commit `0ed9184` has `validate` completed successfully and the trusted `publish` job has completed dependency install, masking, and registry login; it is currently still on `Publish runner image` with the later image/manifest steps pending
- task is not yet complete in this context window because the live hosted publish run has not finished, so `<passes>true</passes>` was not set, task-switch/final commit/push were not done, and the next immediate step is to keep polling run `24640015556`, inspect final publish logs when available, and only finalize if the hosted publish job fully succeeds without credential leaks

- resumed story-21 task 01 from saved progress and confirmed the task still points at the existing three-image publish workflow plan marked NOW EXECUTE
- verified local task metadata is only partially updated so far; the key unknown is whether the last authenticated hosted publish run on main fully completed green
- next step is to inspect the current git commit and real GitHub workflow/run status, then either finalize the task if hosted verification is green or continue fixing the workflow if it is not

- confirmed HEAD is still commit 0ed9184 on branch main and origin/main matches; old progress referenced GitHub run 24640015556 for this commit
- GitHub plugin commit-run helper returned no useful push-run data, so switched to the authenticated shell wrapper as required for honest hosted workflow inspection
- next step is to query the publish-images workflow runs on main directly through the GitHub Actions API, then inspect the latest run/jobs/logs to determine whether task 01 is actually done

- inspected the live hosted run directly via the authenticated GitHub Actions API: run 24640015556 for commit 0ed9184 still reports in_progress, with validate green and publish currently stuck or still running at step `Publish runner image`
- checked the jobs endpoint and confirmed publish has not yet reached setup-sql, verify, or manifest publication, so hosted verification is not complete and the task cannot be finalized yet
- next step is to read the current publish workflow and contract-test boundary again, then either wait for the hosted run to resolve or patch a likely cause if the workflow shape still looks wrong

- re-read the publish workflow and contract-test surface while waiting; static inspection does not yet show a clear contract bug, and the publish job is performing full multi-arch pushes with direct shell-installed buildx as designed
- because the live job is still on the first image publish step, checking the image Dockerfiles now to judge whether this is plausibly just a long cross-arch build or whether the workflow shape is likely to hang indefinitely
- next step is one more hosted status poll after inspecting the Dockerfiles; if it still does not move, I will decide whether evidence is strong enough to patch for buildx/qemu setup rather than waiting blindly

- gave the hosted publish run a longer wait window because the first image push is a full multi-arch build under buildx and may simply be slow rather than misconfigured
- inspected the Dockerfiles during the wait; the runner/setup-sql images compile inside architecture-specific builder stages, which makes a slow arm64 leg plausible and does not by itself imply a wrong workflow shape
- next step is to read the longer poll results and decide whether the run truly progressed, completed, or is stalled hard enough that the workflow needs another TDD slice

- reran required local gates while waiting on hosted verification: `make check` is green again and `make test` is in progress
- hosted run 24640015556 still has validate green and publish stuck or still running on `Publish runner image`; no completed-run log archive exists yet for that publish job
- next step is to finish `make test`, keep polling the hosted run, and only finalize task metadata if both the local gates and the hosted main-push evidence are fully green

- `make check` is green again and `make test` continues to run green through the default suite; no local regression surfaced in this closure pass so far
- the live hosted publish run still has not moved past `Publish runner image`, so hosted verification remains the only unresolved done gate for task 01
- next step is continued polling; if the hosted publish still fails to resolve after the local suite finishes, I will reassess whether that is just long-running image compilation or strong enough evidence for another workflow fix slice

- `make test` completed green end-to-end in this context window, so all required local default lanes now pass again once `make lint` finishes
- task closure is fully blocked on the authenticated hosted publish verification: run 24640015556 still needs to prove the real main-push image publication path succeeds, and the next poll should show whether the long first image build finally completed or is genuinely wedged
- next step is to read the latest hosted poll and either finalize everything if the run turns green or convert the hosted evidence into the next workflow fix slice if it does not

- all required local gates are green again in this window: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed cleanly
- no additional code edits were justified yet because the only unresolved acceptance criterion is the real hosted publish run, and current evidence still fits a long-running multi-arch build more than a confirmed workflow-shape bug
- next step is a longer hosted poll; if the run finishes green I can finalize immediately, and if it still has not resolved then I need to treat the hosted publish behavior itself as the next bug to fix

- finished all required local default lanes green again and entered a longer hosted wait window because the main-push publish run is still the only unresolved acceptance criterion
- no code change was made during this window because there is still no completed hosted failure artifact to target; changing the workflow blind would violate the evidence-first TDD loop
- next step is to read the long-poll result for run 24640015556 and decide between finalization or another real hosted-debug slice

- added one local no-cache timing probe on the runner image to calibrate expectations: a cold single-arch runner image build completed in about 56 seconds on this machine
- that timing makes the hosted run remaining stuck on `Publish runner image` for far longer much more suspicious; it now looks more like a real publish-path problem than just normal compilation time
- next step is to read the latest hosted poll and decide whether this is enough evidence to treat the publish step itself as the next bug to fix

- converted the hosted publish suspicion into a real TDD slice: added a failing workflow contract that requires explicit multi-arch builder proof and plain buildx progress, then patched the workflow green with binfmt registration, a builder-platform assertion step, and `--progress plain`
- targeted contract test is green; rerunning the full local gates now before pushing another incremental main commit to obtain honest hosted verification on the updated publish path
- next step is to get `make check` and `make test` green on this slice, commit just the workflow/test changes, push to main, and inspect the new hosted run instead of the old stuck one

- implemented and locally validated the next hosted-debug slice: workflow now installs `binfmt-support`, enables `qemu-aarch64`, asserts buildx exposes both required platforms before any publish step, and uses `--progress plain` for the image pushes; matching workflow contract coverage was added first and driven red/green
- required local lanes passed after the change: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`
- next step is to push this incremental fix commit to `main`, inspect the new hosted publish run, and only do final task closure if the real main-push publish path succeeds end-to-end

- pushed incremental fix commit `4cd5e86` to main after local green; GitHub created run `24640639307` and cancelled the previously stuck run `24640015556`, confirming the workflow concurrency guard is behaving as intended
- the fresh hosted run is now in validate; once it reaches publish, the new `Assert multi-arch builder support` step should convert the previous silent/hanging behavior into either a fast red or a more trustworthy publish attempt
- next step is to poll run `24640639307` until it either fails with actionable hosted evidence or succeeds fully so the task can be finalized honestly

- fresh hosted run `24640639307` is behaving normally so far: old stuck run cancelled via concurrency, new run is in validate on commit `4cd5e86`
- the decisive evidence is still ahead in the publish job; the new multi-arch builder assertion should either fail fast with a concrete hosted red or allow a more trustworthy publish attempt than before
- next step is to poll until validate finishes and the publish job either reaches or fails the new builder-support step

- still waiting on hosted run `24640639307`; validate remains in progress, which is consistent with GitHub re-running the full `make check` and `make test` lanes on the hosted runner before publish is even eligible
- no task metadata has been updated yet because hosted success remains the hard done gate; the next meaningful state change is validate finishing and the publish job starting the new multi-arch builder assertion step
- next step is a longer poll to see whether the run enters publish or fails during hosted validation

- still blocked on the fresh hosted run finishing validate; no new hosted failure artifact has appeared yet, so the main uncertainty remains whether publish will start cleanly once GitHub finishes the default repo gates
- this turn already proved the old stuck run was replaced correctly and the new builder-proof slice is on main; the next useful observation is whether `Assert multi-arch builder support` runs at all
- next step is to read the long-poll result for run `24640639307` and continue from that hosted evidence

- hosted run `24640639307` has now cleared validate and, critically, the new `Assert multi-arch builder support` step passed on GitHub before the image publish began
- that confirms the workflow is no longer silently assuming buildx/qemu readiness; the remaining done gate is the real runner/setup-sql/verify image publish sequence itself
- next step is to keep polling the publish job until it either succeeds fully or fails with a completed log artifact that can be inspected through the new plain-progress output

- run `24640639307` is in the real publish phase after validate and the new multi-arch builder proof both passed on GitHub
- final task closure is now waiting solely on whether the three image publishes and manifest upload finish successfully; if not, this should finally produce a completed hosted failure with actionable plain-progress logs
- next step is to read the publish-phase poll result and either finalize the task or continue with the next hosted-debug slice

- hosted publish is past the new builder-readiness guardrails and is again running the real `Publish runner image` step on run `24640639307`
- the remaining uncertainty is now narrower than before: multi-arch builder setup is confirmed good, so any further issue is in the actual build/push execution path rather than hidden buildx/qemu readiness
- next step is a longer poll on the live publish step; if it still does not move, the next debugging slice will need to target the runner image build/push itself using the now-available plain-progress evidence

- still waiting on the live runner image publish for run `24640639307`; all readiness and validation gates are already green, so the only unresolved acceptance criterion is the actual image-publish sequence finishing successfully
- if the step still does not move after this wait, the remaining hosted problem is definitively narrowed to the runner image build/push execution itself rather than validation or buildx setup
- next step is to read the long-poll result and decide whether we finally have either a green publish or enough hosted evidence to treat the publish step itself as the next bug

- live job logs are still unavailable mid-run even with plain progress enabled, but GitHub is at least issuing a real blob redirect now instead of failing the job helper outright; the publish step itself remains the only unresolved execution path
- giving the real runner-image publish one more longer window because it may still just be a slow cold multi-arch build+push on hosted infrastructure; if it still does not resolve after that, the runner image publish path becomes the next concrete bug to isolate
- next step is to read the longer publish poll and then either finalize or plan the next hosted-debug slice around the runner image publish itself

- read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skills
- confirmed the active task file is .ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split.md
- next step is to read that task file and determine whether this turn must plan or execute

- read the active task file and its linked plan; the plan is already in NOW EXECUTE and all local execution checkboxes inside the plan are marked done
- checked repo state: HEAD is 4cd5e86 on main and the worktree contains unrelated ralph/task-file churn that should not be reverted blindly
- next step is to inspect the real hosted workflow status for commit 4cd5e86 because hosted main-push verification is still the hard done gate

- authenticated GitHub inspection confirms the latest trusted main-push run is still run 24640639307 on commit 4cd5e86
- validate succeeded; publish is still stuck on step `Publish runner image` after setup, multi-arch assertion, masking, and registry login all passed
- next step is to probe for live job logs or other GitHub-exposed runtime evidence so the stuck publish step can be converted into a concrete bug rather than guesswork

- tested both GitHub job-log and run-log endpoints for the stuck publish job; they redirect to a blob URL that currently returns `404 BlobNotFound`, so live hosted stdout is still unavailable
- the actionable evidence remains: hosted run 24640639307 is stuck specifically in `Publish runner image` while all pre-publish steps already passed
- next step is to inspect the Dockerfiles and workflow-contract tests to find the likely technical cause and drive the next fix slice with TDD rather than guesswork

- ran a local multi-arch buildx probe for the runner image using the same Dockerfile path; it did not reproduce a hang, but it failed almost immediately with `no space left on device` while unpacking the heavy `rust:1.93-bookworm` base layers
- that local failure is different from the hosted stuck publish, but it confirms the current containerized multi-arch build path is heavyweight and brittle enough to justify a deeper refactor instead of more superficial workflow polling
- next step is to inspect whether the published runner tag exists at all on GHCR and then design a TDD slice that makes the image build path lighter and more deterministic on hosted runners

- gave the hosted publish run a longer wait window; it is still unchanged after roughly half an hour on `Publish runner image`, so the current image-build path is now being treated as the next concrete bug rather than just a slow step
- local blocker is cleared after pruning only Docker build cache, restoring about 12G free on `/` for repo lanes and further probes
- next step is to verify the feasible cross-build shape for the Rust images so the next TDD slice targets the build boundary with a concrete, testable fix

- drove a real red/green slice around the workspace TLS dependency boundary: added a contract that rustls and tokio-rustls must disable default crypto features and stay explicitly on the ring backend
- greened that slice by updating Cargo.toml; cargo tree now shows `aws-lc-sys` is no longer in the runner dependency graph, which should materially lighten the hosted Rust image build path even though cross-compiling musl still needs a target C compiler for ring
- next step is to run the required local lanes (`make check`, `make test`, and the lint alias) on this slice, then push and inspect a fresh trusted main-push publish run
